auto_fail_reason: submitted_at is absent — required field per schema and explicit auto-fail condition. jurisdiction is absent — required field per schema and explicit auto-fail condition. Two auto-fail conditions triggered simultaneously.
decision_commentary: Completeness scored 1: rubric schema requires submitted_at, jurisdiction, status, corrections (array), approved_at. The file contains only status='approved', municipal_permit_approved='2026-11-01', approved_at='2026-11-01', and corrections=[]. Both submitted_at and jurisdiction are absent — these are required fields and auto-fail conditions. The file also conflates municipal_permit_approved with approved_at (both same date) but lacks the submission tracking. Score 1: two required fields missing. Accuracy scored 3: jurisdiction should match project location (Los Cabos, Baja California Sur — 'Municipio de Los Cabos — Desarrollo Urbano'). While the jurisdiction is absent, the status='approved' with approved_at='2026-11-01' is plausible timing given construction start='2026-09-15' in the schedule — actually the permit is approved AFTER construction start, which is a logical inconsistency. However scoring accuracy 3 because the permit approval date and corrections tracking are otherwise internally consistent (empty corrections, approved status). Clarity scored 3: status='approved' is unambiguous and Vera could trigger construction from this; however without submitted_at, the permit tracking history is lost. State_sync scored 3: simulated run. Timing scored 5: permit produced in correct pipeline position.
celia_routing_notes: No Celia routing on permit-status — Paco fires after contractor selection, Vera auto-unlocks on approval.
